name: Gemini Pro / Imagen 4 / Veo / Whisk for App Assets
definition_origin: Gemini Pro (also called Gemini Advanced) is Google's paid Gemini app subscription (~$20/mo) that bundles state-of-the-art generative AI: Imagen 4 (text-to-image, photorealistic + illustration), Veo 3.1 (text-to-video, 8s 720p/1080p with native audio), Gems (custom-instructed assistants with knowledge files and Drive integration), and NotebookLM (research synthesis from uploaded sources). Whisk was Google Labs' image-remix tool (subject + scene + style references → new image) that ran 2023→April 2026 — its capabilities migrated into Flow as of February 25, 2026 unification. For a solo broke dev who already has Gemini Pro, this is the most leveraged $20/mo in the AI stack: it covers static images, video, brand-consistent generation, and research synthesis in one subscription.
core_purpose: Gemini Pro is the user's central asset creation engine. Imagen 4 covers visceral (hero illustrations, mascots, app store screenshots), Veo covers behavioral storytelling (app preview videos, marketing demos), Whisk → Flow covers reflective brand iteration (try the same character in 5 moods/lighting setups), Gems enforces brand consistency at scale (every generation uses the brand palette + archetype + negative-prompt automatically), and NotebookLM grounds prompts in the brand bible (so the AI doesn't drift into generic SaaS aesthetics). Together they turn a designless dev into someone shipping coherent branded assets.
when_needed: Consumer mobile (Kleeve-style emotional apps): Imagen 4 for onboarding illustrations + mascot art + empty states; Veo for App Store preview + landing-page demo. B2B/dev-tool: Imagen 4 for blog headers + empty states; Gems-powered brand consistency. AI-wrapper: Imagen 4 for assistant avatar + landing hero; Veo for product-demo b-roll. Marketing surfaces: full stack — Imagen 4 hero + Veo demo + NotebookLM-driven copy + Gems-driven brand consistency.
format_options: Imagen 4 outputs PNG/JPG (1024x1024 standard, up to 2048x2048 in some surfaces). For SVG, vectorize downstream (Recraft free tier, Inkscape Trace Bitmap). Veo 3.1 outputs MP4 (8s, 720p / 1080p / 4K, 16:9 landscape or 9:16 portrait, with native audio in Veo 3+). Veo 3.1 Lite is the 2026 cost-efficient model. Whisk (until April 2026) outputted PNG; Flow (its successor) handles MP4 + PNG. Gems output text + image generation contextualized by the Gem's instruction + knowledge files. NotebookLM outputs text/notes/podcasts/infographics from sources.
file_size_performance: Imagen 4 PNG outputs are ~2-4MB at 1024x1024 — convert to WebP via Squoosh for ~80% reduction before shipping. For app screenshots (PNG required by stores), use TinyPNG/pngquant for lossless compression. Veo MP4 outputs are ~8-30MB for 8s 1080p — use HandBrake or ffmpeg with H.264 + 2-pass to reach <3MB for App Store / Play Store preview videos. Veo 3.1 Lite is <50% of Veo 3.1 Fast cost at same speed — use Lite for high-volume iteration, full Veo 3.1 only for final.
free_tools: Gemini Pro is paid ($20/mo) but the user has it. Adjacent free tools that complement: 1) **Google ImageFX** (labs.google/fx/tools/image-fx) — free Imagen 3 access without Gemini Pro for non-subscribers. 2) **Google AI Studio** (aistudio.google.com) — free programmatic access to Veo 3 + Imagen via API with limited quota. 3) **Recraft free tier** — vectorize Imagen 4 PNG outputs to SVG (~15 SVG/day). 4) **Squoosh.app** — free WebP/AVIF/PNG converter for Imagen outputs. 5) **HandBrake / ffmpeg** — free MP4 compression for Veo outputs. 6) **NotebookLM** — free tier (with usage limits); Gemini Pro upgrades quotas. 7) **Vectorizer.ai** — free trial for SVG conversion of Imagen 4 outputs.
paid_tools: Starter $10-15/mo: **Gemini Pro / Advanced** (~$19.99/mo) — the user already has this. **Recraft Starter** (~$12/mo) — for production-grade SVG vectorization with private + commercial. **Ideogram Basic** ($8/mo) — when Imagen 4's text rendering isn't enough. Pro $20-30/mo: **Adobe Firefly Pro** (~$22/mo) — commercial-safe + IP indemnification when shipping under contract. **Midjourney Basic** ($10/mo) — alternative aesthetic to Imagen 4. Enterprise $50+/mo: **Google Workspace + Gemini for Business** — team Gems with shared knowledge, brand assets stored centrally. For solo broke dev: Gemini Pro alone covers ~85% of needs; add Recraft Starter ($12/mo) only if SVG output is daily.
gemini_pro_workflow:
**FULL WORKFLOW for solo dev with Gemini Pro:**

**Step 0 — Brand Bible (one-time, 60 min):** Write a 1-page brand-bible doc (Google Doc): name, archetype (Caregiver/Magician/Jester/etc.), 5-7 hex palette, character description if mascot, voice (formal/playful/etc.), anti-slop negative prompt list, 3 reference apps you admire and why.

**Step 1 — Build Brand-Asset Gem (one-time, 30 min):** In Gemini app → Gems → Create. Instructions: 'You are the brand-asset generator for [App Name]. Every image prompt must include the palette {hex_list}, archetype mood {Caregiver/etc.}, style modifier {flat-design vector / soft pastel / etc.}, and the negative prompt {no isometric, no chibi, no gradient blob, no generic SaaS purple-blue, no melted features, no 3D voxel}. When the user describes an asset need, output 3 prompt variations refined for Imagen 4.' Knowledge: upload brand-bible doc + this research file.

**Step 2 — NotebookLM Brand-Research (one-time, 30 min):** Create a NotebookLM notebook 'App Asset Research'. Sources: brand-bible doc + Duolingo shape-language post + Headspace design articles + this research file + lottie/rive runtime docs. Use to ask: 'Given my Caregiver archetype, what 5 illustrations should I prioritize for onboarding?' or 'What motion principles match my brand voice?'

**Step 3 — Generate static art with Imagen 4:** In Gemini app, ask the Brand-Asset Gem 'I need a hero illustration for [feature]' → Gem returns 3 prompt variations → run each through Imagen 4 → pick best, iterate. Imagen 4 prompt template below.

**Step 4 — Iterate moods with Whisk → Flow (post-April 2026: use Flow):** Combine subject + scene + style references to remix the same character/scene across moods. Useful for mascot variations (idle/happy/sad/surprised) where you need consistent proportions.

**Step 5 — Generate video with Veo 3.1 / Veo 3.1 Lite:** For app preview videos, use Veo 3.1 Lite for iteration (cheaper) → Veo 3.1 full for final. Prompt template below. Always 9:16 portrait for App Store.

**Step 6 — Vectorize and Optimize:** Imagen PNGs → Recraft vectorize for SVG (icons/logos) → SVGO. Imagen PNGs → Squoosh for WebP. Veo MP4s → HandBrake → <3MB target.

**Step 7 — Drop into codebase:** Imagen outputs → apps/mobile/src/assets/images/. Vectorized SVGs → apps/mobile/src/assets/icons/. Veo MP4 → marketing site (web/public/) or Fastlane App Store metadata.

**KEY SUPER-PROMPTS:**

IMAGEN 4 (illustration): 'A {flat-design vector|soft pastel|geometric architectural} illustration of {subject} for a {brand_archetype} mobile app called {app_name}. Limited 5-color palette: #X #Y #Z #W #K. Hard edges, no gradients except minimal linear. Generous whitespace. Centered composition. Style references: {Duolingo / Headspace / Linear}. Photorealism: NO. Isometric: NO. Chibi: NO. Generic SaaS aesthetic: NO. Melted features: NO.'

IMAGEN 4 (mascot): 'A {animal/character} mascot named {name} with {personality_trait} expression for a {app_category} app. Flat-design 2D vector, identical proportions to provided reference, palette #X #Y #Z, transparent background. Pose: {idle|celebrating|thinking|sad|surprised}. NO chibi, NO 3D render, NO generic SaaS, NO melted features. Style: {Duolingo Duo|Headspace illustrations|Slack avatars}.'

IMAGEN 4 (app store screenshot): 'iPhone 15 Pro displayed at 9:16 portrait, screen showing {app_name} {feature} UI mockup. Hands not visible. Soft natural studio lighting, clean {brand_archetype}-mood background palette #X #Y #Z. Text on screen reads exactly: "{exact_words}". Photorealistic device, illustrated background.'

VEO 3.1 (app preview): 'A 6-second 9:16 portrait video. iPhone 15 Pro centered, screen displays {app_name} {feature_flow}: scene 1 (0-2s) {state}, scene 2 (2-4s) thumb taps {element}, scene 3 (4-6s) success state. Hand visible only at moments of touch. Soft natural studio lighting. No text overlays. Camera fixed.'

WHISK / FLOW (mood iteration): 'Subject: {previous Imagen 4 mascot output}. Scene: {bedroom / outdoor / office}. Style: {watercolor / flat-design / oil painting}. Output: same character, new context.'

GEMS (NEW BRAND-VOICE GEM): 'You are the brand voice for {app_name}, a {archetype} app. All copy uses {voice} tone. Never use {banned_words}. Always end with {call_to_action}.' Knowledge: brand bible.
workflow_steps: 1) (One-time) Write brand bible — palette, archetype, character, anti-slop negative list (60 min). 2) (One-time) Create Brand-Asset Gem with bible as knowledge file (30 min). 3) (One-time) Create NotebookLM 'App Assets' notebook with bible + reference articles (30 min). 4) For each asset: ask Gem for prompt variations (3 min). 5) Run prompt in Imagen 4, generate 4-8 variations (3 min). 6) Pick best, iterate prompt 1-2x (10 min). 7) If video needed: write Veo prompt, generate with Veo 3.1 Lite first for iteration (5 min), then Veo 3.1 full for final (5 min). 8) Optimize: Squoosh for WebP, HandBrake for MP4, SVGO for vectorized SVG (5 min). 9) Drop into codebase + add accessibility metadata (5 min). 10) Test on device + verify brand consistency vs prior assets (10 min). Total: ~30-60 min per polished asset, MUCH faster than designer + roundtrip.
ai_prompt_templates: 1) **Imagen 4 illustration (universal)**: 'A flat-design vector illustration of {subject} for a {archetype} mobile app. Palette: {hex_list}. Hard edges, generous whitespace, centered composition. Style refs: Duolingo, Headspace. NO photorealism, NO isometric, NO chibi, NO gradient blob, NO generic SaaS aesthetic, NO melted features.' 2) **Imagen 4 mascot pose set**: 'Five identical-proportion poses of {character_name}, a {animal/type} mascot for {app_name}. Palette {hex_list}. Pose 1: idle. Pose 2: happy. Pose 3: thinking. Pose 4: surprised. Pose 5: sad. Same lighting, same body proportions in all 5.' 3) **Imagen 4 App Store screenshot**: 'iPhone 15 Pro photographed at 9:16, screen showing {app_name} {feature} UI. Text on screen reads "{exact_words}". Background: soft {archetype}-mood gradient {hex_list}. Hand not visible.' 4) **Veo 3.1 app preview**: '6-second 9:16 portrait. iPhone 15 Pro showing {app_name} {feature}: scene 1 {state}, scene 2 thumb tap, scene 3 success. Soft studio lighting, no text overlays, camera fixed.' 5) **Whisk/Flow style remix**: 'Subject: [my mascot image]. Scene: [new context]. Style: [art style ref].' 6) **Gem instruction template**: 'You are [role]. Every output must include [palette/voice/archetype]. Never produce [banned items]. When user requests [thing], output [3 variations / structured response].' 7) **NotebookLM brand-bible query**: 'Given my [archetype] brand and these reference apps, what visual motifs should be present in onboarding illustrations?' 8) **Anti-slop negative prompt baseline**: 'no isometric, no chibi, no gradient blob, no generic SaaS purple-blue, no melted features, no 3D voxel render, no random floating geometric shapes, no waxy AI skin, no extra fingers.'
claude_code_integration: Claude Code helps integrate Gemini-generated assets cleanly: scaffold a `scripts/regenerate-assets.ts` that hits Gemini API + Imagen 4 + writes to apps/mobile/src/assets/ + auto-runs Squoosh/SVGO. Maintain a `.agent/asset-prompts.json` catalog of prompts per asset for reproducibility. For Veo videos, scaffold Fastlane snapshot config or Expo asset bundling. Component pattern: Expo Image for raster, react-native-svg-transformer for SVG, react-native-video / expo-av for MP4. Claude Code can also generate the alt-text for accessibility from the image filename + context. Add a pre-commit hook that checks all images in src/assets/ are <300KB (raster) or <50KB (SVG) and fails CI if violated.
emotional_design_integration: Imagen 4 + Gem-enforced palette is the brand-archetype machine. Caregiver: prompt modifier 'soft pastel palette {pastel_hex_list}, gentle organic curves, generous whitespace, no sharp edges, watercolor texture optional' → Imagen 4 produces Headspace-class output. Magician: 'deep navy palette with single bright accent, geometric architectural composition, dramatic side lighting, mysterious atmosphere' → Linear-class. Jester: 'saturated primaries {hex_list}, bouncy exaggerated proportions, playful expressions' → Duolingo-class. Hero: 'bold contrast palette, dynamic action lines, low-angle dramatic perspective.' Sage: 'muted earth tones, restrained composition, intellectual minimalism, NYT/Stripe Press style.' Outlaw: 'high-contrast palette, glitchy edges, asymmetric composition.' The same Imagen 4 model produces wildly different aesthetics from these modifiers — your Gem locks one set so every gen is on-brand.
specific_examples: 1) **Anthropic Claude marketing pages** — Imagen-class illustrations with disciplined palette, used as in-product illustrations and marketing heroes. 2) **Bolt.new / v0.dev** — clearly AI-generated marketing assets but tightly brand-controlled. 3) **Linear (linear.app)** — generated heroes at the Magician end of the spectrum (deep navy + cyan accent). https://linear.app 4) **Headspace** — illustrated style that Imagen 4 + soft-pastel modifier reproduces well. https://headspace.com 5) **Duolingo Duo** — Imagen 4 + 'flat-design owl mascot, green and yellow palette' replicates the style. https://blog.duolingo.com/shape-language-duolingos-art-style/ 6) **Cluely / Linear-style mascots** — static art generated by AI then animated in Rive (engineering-interactive-mascots dev.to writeup).
anti_ai_slop_traps: Gemini-specific slop tells: (a) **Imagen 4 photorealistic 'people working at laptops in coffee shops'** — instant stock-AI tell. Counter: never request photorealism for illustration assets; lock to flat-design + vector. (b) **Imagen 4 default 'detailed illustration' aesthetic** drifts toward greeting-card style. Counter: explicit reference apps in prompt + tight palette. (c) **Veo 3.1 cinematic-overdrive** — every generated video has dramatic camera moves and warm sunset lighting. Counter: explicit 'fixed camera, soft natural lighting, no camera moves'. (d) **Veo melted-UI-mockups** — when asked to show app screens, Veo invents fake UI that doesn't match real product. Counter: use Veo only for B-roll/ambience, screen-record real app for the actual UI showcase, composite in CapCut. (e) **Gem-drift over time** — your Gem's outputs slowly drift away from brand without retraining. Counter: keep brand bible doc updated, periodically re-upload to Gem knowledge. (f) **Whisk/Flow remix-mush** — combining 3 unrelated style refs creates AI-slop chimera. Counter: keep one style ref, vary subject and scene only. UNIVERSAL ANTI-SLOP: 1) Build the Gem with anti-slop negative prompts baked in. 2) Generate 4-8 variations, ship 1 in 10. 3) 'Embarrassed if a designer saw this' test before shipping. 4) Periodically compare new gens vs first gens — drift means retrain Gem.
common_pitfalls: 1) Not building a Gem first — every prompt is reinvented, brand drifts. 2) Burning daily Imagen 4 quota on bad prompts — write + iterate prompts in chat first, generate only when prompt is sharp. 3) Over-relying on Imagen 4 photorealism — feels stock-AI; use illustration mode with flat-design lock. 4) Veo for app UI demos — invents fake UI; use only for ambience/b-roll. 5) Ignoring Veo cost: Veo 3.1 full is expensive even on Pro; use Lite for iteration. 6) Whisk shutting down April 2026 → migrate to Flow; some saved creations may need manual recovery [verify Flow migration]. 7) Storing brand bible only in Gem instructions — version-control the doc instead, re-upload as it evolves. 8) Treating NotebookLM as a chatbot — its strength is grounded synthesis from your sources, not free-form generation.
accessibility_notes: Add alt text to every Imagen 4 generated image (Claude Code can auto-generate from prompt + filename). For Veo videos, provide captions track (.vtt) and a text description; respect prefers-reduced-motion by serving a static poster image fallback. For mascot characters, ensure expressions are unambiguous on small screens. Color contrast: WCAG AA 4.5:1 for any text overlaid on Imagen-generated background. For state-conveying mascots (e.g. 'thinking') pair with visible text label.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Imagen 4 outputs (static raster) are platform-agnostic — convert to WebP and use Expo Image on mobile, <picture> on web. Veo MP4 outputs: mobile uses react-native-video / expo-av; web uses <video> with WebM (transcoded via ffmpeg) for better compression. Aspect ratio matters: 9:16 portrait for App Store / TikTok / Reels; 16:9 landscape for YouTube / web hero; 1:1 for Instagram. Generate at correct aspect from Veo (it supports both 9:16 and 16:9 natively).
sources: 1) [Gemini Veo 3.1 docs](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/video). 2) [Veo 3.1 Lite announcement](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/ai/veo-3-1-lite/). 3) [Whisk shutdown / Flow migration](https://nativeai.agency/hub/tools/whisk/). 4) [Gemini Gems overview](https://gemini.google/overview/gems/). 5) [NotebookLM brand-bible workflow](https://marketingagent.blog/2026/02/05/the-complete-guide-to-using-notebook-lm-for-marketing-in-2026/). 6) [Imagen 4 Ultra prompting guide](https://www.atlabs.ai/blog/imagen-4-prompting-guide). 7) [Google Cloud: brand consistency with Gemini](https://cloud.google.com/transform/closing-the-creative-gap-how-gemini-supports-brand-consistency). 8) [ImageFX free access](https://labs.google/fx/tools/image-fx). 9) [Mastering Negative Prompts 2026](https://www.gensgpt.com/blog/mastering-negative-prompts-ai-image-generation-2026). 10) [Duolingo shape language reference](https://blog.duolingo.com/shape-language-duolingos-art-style/).
